WPEPlatform

API Version: 2.0

Library Version: 2.52.1

Type
View
Methods
buffer_released buffer_rendered buffers_changed closed compute_press_count event focus_in focus_out get_accessible get_display get_gesture_controller get_has_focus get_height get_mapped get_preferred_buffer_formats get_scale get_screen get_toplevel get_toplevel_state get_visible get_width lock_pointer map render_buffer resized set_cursor_from_bytes set_cursor_from_name set_gesture_controller set_opaque_rectangles set_toplevel set_visible unlock_pointer unmap

Generated by gi-docgen 2023.3

Method

WPEPlatformViewrender_buffer

Declaration [src] 

gboolean
wpe_view_render_buffer (
  WPEView* view,
  WPEBuffer* buffer,
  const WPERectangle* damage_rects,
  guint n_damage_rects,
  GError** error
)

Description [src]

Render the given buffer into view. If this function returns TRUE you must call wpe_view_buffer_rendered() when the buffer is rendered and wpe_view_buffer_released() when it’s no longer used by the view.

Parameters

buffer

Type: WPEBuffer

A WPEBuffer to render.

The data is owned by the caller of the method.
damage_rects

Type: An array of WPERectangle

Damage rectangles.

The argument can be NULL.
The length of the array is specified in the n_damage_rects argument.
The data is owned by the caller of the method.
n_damage_rects

Type: guint

Number of rectangles in damage_rects.

error

Type: GError **

The return location for a recoverable error.

The argument can be NULL.
If the return location is not NULL, then you must initialize it to a NULL GError*.
The argument will be left initialized to NULL by the method if there are no errors.
In case of error, the argument will be set to a newly allocated GError; the caller will take ownership of the data, and be responsible for freeing it.

Return value

Type: gboolean

TRUE if buffer will be rendered, or FALSE otherwise.
